title: HMAS HOBART in eva
wikitext:
[]: HMAS HOBART in eva             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:35 am I found several simmelarities Between the New AUD's the Navy is getting compaired to the Ship that unit 02 jumps on the front of. From front to back. Main Gun. Small gun just before the bridge. That Hexigon style amered plating below the bridge and behind. The gun mount to the side of the bridge. The mast position and the radar dish ontop of the bridge. 2 exaust towers. 2nd tower has areial on it. 2 radar dishes and small gun on rear of ship. I beleave where looking at the first design of an awd. Further more the Adelaide class FFG's (HMAS: SYDNEY, DARWIN, MELBOUREN and NEWCASTLE) are going to be replaced by the new AWD's and will be in commition around this time. Makes me happy to see that australians may have had an involvment in eva ;) Don't link images from THAT BOARD, it's an image board so the image will disappear in a week or so anyways

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:43 am Nope, sorry. It's the USS Ramage , an Arleigh Burke -class guided missile destroyer launched in 1994.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:46 am wow I thaut they would have all been decimmitioned by then the ddg's are old as hell. Well thats cool to know might do a tad little fan editing :pyro:

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:51 am Zeak wrote: wow I thaut they would have all been decimmitioned by then the ddg's are old as hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:57 am Guess so, It also says High Degree of Interoperability with US Navy. Well guess we do get most of our stuff from the states the FFG's called the Adelaide class here are the Perry class in the states. Funny though we had the side number 01 to 06 and the USA had 07 onwoods. but Adelaide and Canberra are Now Decommitioned and Canbera was sunk. Adelaid Decommitioned on the 28th of Jan. Lol see what we did to our DDG's type HMAS TORRENS into youtube, and you can see what a torpedo realy does to a ship lol.

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Sun Feb 17, 2008 4:34 am BobBQ wrote: Ramage was brand new when NGE was in production. Also, the Pacific Fleet shown in episode eight includes no less than four Iowa -class battleships, all built in the 1940s, and Kirov -class battlecruisers constructed in the 1980s. Having the very latest and greatest doesn't seem to be an urgent priority.

From front to back. Main Gun. Small gun just before the bridge. That Hexigon style amered plating below the bridge and behind. The gun mount to the side of the bridge. I beleave where looking at the first design of an awd. Further more the Adelaide class FFG's (HMAS: SYDNEY, DARWIN, MELBOUREN and NEWCASTLE) are going to be replaced by the new AWD's and will be in commition around this time.
